Holograms#

CGH#

class QHOT.lib.holograms.CGH.CGH(*, shape=(512, 512), wavelength=1.064, n_m=1.34, magnification=100.0, focallength=200.0, camerapitch=4.8, slmpitch=8.0, scale=3.0, splay=0.01, xs=0.0, ys=0.0, phis=8.0, xc=320.0, yc=240.0, zc=0.0, thetac=0.0, parent=None)[source]#

Bases: QObject

Base class for computing computer-generated holograms.

For each trap, the coordinate r obtained from the fabscreen is measured relative to the calibrated location rc of the zeroth-order focal point, which itself is measured relative to the center of the focal plane. The resulting displacement is projected onto the coordinate system in the SLM plane via a calibrated rotation matrix.

The hologram is computed using calibrated wavenumbers for the Cartesian coordinates in the SLM plane. These differ from each other because the SLM is likely to be tilted relative to the optical axis.

Assigning any calibration attribute automatically triggers updateGeometry or updateTransformationMatrix as appropriate, and emits the recalculate signal.

dtype#

NumPy dtype used for complex field arrays. Defaults to np.complex128. Subclasses (e.g. GPU-accelerated variants) may override this to use an alternative complex type.

Type:

type

phase#

Quantized phase hologram from the most recent compute() call. Undefined before the first call to compute().

Type:

np.ndarray

shape#

Hologram dimensions (height, width) in pixels.

Type:

tuple[int, int]

wavelength#

Vacuum wavelength of trapping light [μm].

Type:

float

n_m#

Refractive index of the trapping medium.

Type:

float

magnification#

Magnification of the objective lens.

Type:

float

focallength#

Focal length of the tube lens [μm].

Type:

float

camerapitch#

Camera pixel pitch [μm/pixel].

Type:

float

slmpitch#

SLM pixel pitch [μm/phixel].

Type:

float

scale#

SLM scale factor.

Type:

float

splay#

Axial splay correction factor (dimensionless).

Type:

float

xs, ys

Coordinates of the optical axis in the SLM plane [phixels].

Type:

float

phis#

Tilt of the SLM relative to the optical axis [degrees].

Type:

float

xc, yc, zc

Coordinates of the optical axis in the camera plane [pixels].

Type:

float

thetac#

Rotation of the camera relative to the SLM [degrees].

Type:

float

Signals#
-------
hologramReady#

Emitted with the quantized phase array when a hologram is computed.

Type:

QtCore.pyqtSignal(np.ndarray)

recalculate#

Emitted when the geometry or transformation matrix is updated.

Type:

QtCore.pyqtSignal()

hologramReady#

Emitted with the quantized phase array when a hologram is computed.

recalculate#

Emitted when the geometry or transformation matrix is updated.

dtype#

alias of complex64

updateTransformationMatrix()[source]#

Rebuild the camera-to-SLM transformation matrix.

Accounts for the position (xc, yc, zc) and orientation (thetac) of the camera relative to the SLM. Clears only the field cache (structure arrays are independent of the transformation) and emits recalculate.

Return type:

None

updateGeometry()[source]#

Recompute position-dependent phase factors in the SLM plane.

Rebuilds iqx, iqy, iqxz, iqyz, theta, and qr from the current calibration parameters. Also resets the accumulation field buffer, clears the field cache, and emits recalculate.

Return type:

None

property properties: list[str]#

Names of all calibration attributes.

Returns:

list[str] – Ordered list of calibration attribute names.

property settings: dict[str, object]#

Current calibration settings as a dictionary.

Returns:

dict[str, object] – Mapping of calibration attribute name to current value.

property height: int#

Height of the hologram in pixels.

property width: int#

Width of the hologram in pixels.

property rc: QVector3D#

Coordinates of optical axis in camera plane.

Returns:

QtGui.QVector3D – Position (xc, yc, zc) as a 3D vector.

property wavenumber: float#

Wavenumber of trapping light in the medium [radians/μm].

Returns:

float – 2π n_m / wavelength.

property qprp: float#

In-plane displacement factor [radians/(pixel·phixel)].

Returns:

float – Lateral phase gradient per unit displacement.

property qpar: float#

Axial displacement factor [radians/(pixel·phixel²)].

Returns:

float – Axial phase gradient per unit displacement.

start()[source]#

Initialize the CGH pipeline and return self.

Return type:

CGH

Returns:

CGH – This instance, for chaining.

stop()[source]#

Shut down the CGH pipeline.

Return type:

None

static quantize(field)[source]#

Scale the phase of a complex field to an 8-bit integer array.

Parameters:

field (Field) – Complex-valued field array.

Return type:

ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[uint8]]

Returns:

Hologram – Phase encoded as uint8 in the range [0, 255].

window(r)[source]#

Compute the sinc-aperture amplitude correction for a trap position.

Parameters:

r (QtGui.QVector3D) – Trap position in SLM coordinates.

Return type:

float

Returns:

float – Amplitude correction factor, clamped to a maximum of 100.

transform(r)[source]#

Map camera-plane coordinates to SLM-plane coordinates.

Applies the calibrated rotation/translation matrix and the axial splay correction.

Parameters:

r (QtGui.QVector3D) – Position in camera coordinates.

Return type:

QVector3D

Returns:

QtGui.QVector3D – Position in SLM coordinates.

fieldOf(trap)[source]#

Compute the complex field contribution of a single trap.

The displacement field and structure field are cached separately. trap.changed invalidates the displacement field cache; trap.structureChanged (if present) invalidates only the structure cache, leaving the displacement field intact.

Parameters:

trap (QTrap) – The trap to compute the field for.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

Field – Complex field array with shape equal to self.shape.

compute(traps)[source]#

Compute the phase hologram for a list of traps.

For traps belonging to a group, the group’s accumulated complex field is used directly (and updated in place by _onGroupMoved on each translation). The group field is built lazily on the first compute call after creation or cache invalidation. Ungrouped traps use the existing per-trap fieldOf cache.

Parameters:

traps (list[QTrap]) – Traps to include in the hologram.

Return type:

ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[uint8]]

Returns:

Hologram – Quantized phase hologram as a uint8 array.

bless(field)[source]#

Cast a field array to self.dtype, or return None.

Parameters:

field (Field or None) – Array to cast, or None.

Return type:

ndarray | None

Returns:

Field or None – Field cast to self.dtype, or None if input is None.

classmethod example()[source]#

Demonstrate the CGH pipeline.

Creates a CGH instance, computes a hologram for a single on-axis tweezer trap, and prints the result shape and dtype. Can be called on any subclass.

Return type:

None

QCGHTree#

class QHOT.lib.holograms.QCGHTree.QCGHTree(*args, cgh=None, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: ParameterTree

Parameter tree widget for editing CGH calibration settings.

Displays all CGH calibration parameters grouped by subsystem (instrument, SLM, camera) and synchronises changes bidirectionally with a connected CGH instance.

Parameters:

  • cgh (CGH or None) – The hologram computation object to synchronise with. Can be changed at runtime via the cgh property.

  • *args, **kwargs – Forwarded to ParameterTree.

cgh#

The currently connected CGH instance.

Type:

CGH or None

settings#

Current values of all parameters, keyed by parameter name.

Type:

dict[str, object]

properties#

Names of all registered parameters.

Type:

KeysView[str]

property cgh: CGH | None#

The currently connected CGH instance.

get(key, default=None)[source]#

Return the current value of a parameter by name.

Parameters:

  • key (str) – Parameter name.

  • default (object) – Value to return if the key is not found. Default: None.

Return type:

object

Returns:

object – Current parameter value, or default if not found.

set(key, value)[source]#

Set the value of a parameter by name.

Parameters:

  • key (str) – Parameter name.

  • value (object) – New value to assign.

Return type:

None

property properties: KeysView[str]#

Names of all registered parameters.

property settings: dict[str, object]#

Current values of all parameters as a dict.

updateCGH(tree, changes)[source]#

Slot called when any parameter value changes.

Applies the changed value to the connected CGH instance if the parameter name matches a known CGH field.

Parameters:

  • tree (Parameter) – The parameter tree (unused).

  • changes (list[tuple[Parameter, str, object]]) – List of (param, change, value) tuples emitted by sigTreeStateChanged.

Return type:

None

updateTree()[source]#

Populate the tree with the current CGH settings.

Only parameters that have a corresponding tree entry are applied; CGH fields with no tree representation (e.g. shape) are silently skipped.

Return type:

None

classmethod example()[source]#

Display an interactive CGH parameter tree demo.

Return type:

None
